mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market, which drives higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-COVID labor market is in a sharp recovery phase.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is consistently below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is pinned at the zero-lower bound, it signals maximum monetary accommodation. This lowers the discount rate for future cash flows and encourages risk-taking, boosting equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has signaled rates will remain low for an extended period.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly higher when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% compared to when it is above this threshold (p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong growth in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer strength. This joint signal of consumption on goods and large investments implies robust future corporate revenues, driving equity prices higher.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 1% AND 3-month change in HOUST > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:Fiscal stimulus is directly supporting consumer spending.
failure_condition: Instances where both proxies are growing strongly are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) following a major economic shock indicates that supply chains are healing and production is ramping to meet recovering demand. This alleviates bottleneck fears and supports corporate profit margins, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is rebounding sharply from its April 2020 low.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative during post-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a primary indicator of recession. This signals falling demand, deteriorating corporate earnings, and heightened risk aversion, causing a decline in equity prices.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns, indicating the signal is unreliable.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in the monthly inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) increases the probability of future monetary tightening by the central bank. This leads to higher expected discount rates for equities, causing an immediate negative price reaction.
evidence_refs: fact:Markets are highly sensitive to inflation data for Fed policy clues.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following upside CPI surprises is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained, high year-over-year growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects a robust and expanding economy. This provides a strong fundamental tailwind for aggregate corporate earnings growth, supporting higher equity market valuations over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic health.
failure_condition: The beta of 60-day forward S&P 500 returns to the year-over-year percentage change in GDPC1 is negative or statistically insignificant over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low but inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued, it creates a 'Goldilocks' scenario. The economy is near full employment without triggering a hawkish monetary policy response, which is highly bullish for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Phillips curve relationship has appeared flat in recent years.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 forward return during periods meeting the trigger condition is not statistically higher than the unconditional average return.
